title: captured-signal.v1
description: The VERBATIM raw capture signal teed at the bot's capture bridge BEFORE the pipeline — the exact stream a live bug rides on, stored so it replays through the same pipeline OFFLINE (O-TEL-2). One session = a SessionHeader followed by N records (JSONL-on-disk); a record is a CapturedFrame (audio) or a HintEvent (a `type`-tagged out-of-band speaker hint). Each frame mirrors the @vexa/capture-codec binary frame shape: pcm is base64 of the Float32 PCM bytes (little-endian), so a frame round-trips through the codec encode→decode→same.

Lane.description: Which pipeline lane the frame feeds — gmeet (per-channel, glow-named) or mixed (one stream, named from hints).

HintEvent.description: One OUT-OF-BAND speaker hint as it crossed the capture bridge. The mixed lane (zoom/teams/jitsi) carries a single audio stream and names it from active-speaker hints that arrive on their OWN channel, independent of the audio frames — so a session that stored only CapturedFrames could never reproduce attribution offline. gmeet binds its glow name onto the frame itself and emits no HintEvent. `t` shares the audio frames' epoch-ms clock (the bridge's clock contract), which is what lets a replay re-window the hints against the same audio.

t.description: hint epoch ms — the SAME clock domain as CapturedFrame.ts

name.description: the speaker the platform reports as active

isEnd.description: true when the hint marks the END of that speaker's turn

CapturedFrame.description: One raw capture frame as it crossed the capture-bridge tap. ts is CAPTURE epoch ms (never restamped). speakerIndex is the channel id (999 = mixed, 1000 = the local mic). pcm carries the Float32 PCM bytes base64-encoded; pcm_len is the sample count.

ts.description: CAPTURE epoch ms — carried from the frame, never restamped

speakerIndex.description: CHANNEL id (raw track; 999 = mixed, 1000 = local mic)

speakerName.description: glow name bound AT CAPTURE (gmeet), when known

hint.description: mixed-lane active-speaker hint name, when present

pcm.description: base64 of the Float32 PCM bytes (little-endian) — the codec wire payload

SessionHeader.description: Opens a captured-signal.v1 session — the topology needed to replay it through the right lane. Mirrors the legacy tape header (platform/native/language/startedAt) plus the contract version + sampleRate.

trace_id.description: the distributed trace_id (logevent.v1) of the meeting session this signal was captured under — links the raw signal back to every structured log across gateway→meeting-api→runtime→bot for the same meeting, so a replay-and-fix starts from the exact trace the bug rode on
